title: Analytics

sections.markdown: Firebase Analytics (Google Analytics for Firebase) is an event-based product analytics platform. You log events + parameters; Firebase computes retention, funnels, audiences and forwards data to BigQuery for arbitrary SQL.

rows: Extra fields on an event (value, currency)
Event marked as a business goal

sections.markdown: **Instrument once, keep the schema.** Naming discipline matters: chose stable event names (snake_case) and a small, documented parameter set. Renaming later breaks funnels and BigQuery reports.
Ignoring consent. Under GDPR / DPDPA, log analytics only after explicit consent (or run in a consent-mode configuration). Also: burying PII in parameters — never log emails/phone/tokens.
Enable the free BigQuery export on day one — historical raw events are gold for SQL analysis and stitching with server data. Use DebugView to verify events before releasing.

flashcards.back: Events with parameters + user properties; audiences and conversions derive from them.
Raw event data becomes SQL-queryable for arbitrary analysis and joins with backend data.
Never — no emails, phone, tokens, or personal identifiers. Use pseudonymous IDs.

quiz.explain: Raw events land in BigQuery daily (free).

quiz.q: Consent for analytics in the EU/India is…
quiz.explain: Explicit consent (or Consent Mode) is required.

challenges.text: Log a custom event with parameters; see it in DebugView.
Create a purchase funnel + define a conversion.
Enable BigQuery export and write a retention SQL query.
Implement full consent flow (Consent Mode) for GDPR + DPDPA compliance.

interviewQuestions: Why is event naming so important?
How do you handle PII and consent?
BigQuery export — why turn it on?
